questions.question:
Consider the C code fragment given below.


typedef struct node {
int data;
node* next ;
} node;
void join (node* m, node* n) {
node* p=n ;
while (p->next ! =NULL){
p = p -> next ;
}
p-> next = m;
}
Assuming that m and n point to valid NULL- terminated linked lists, invocation of join will
options:
A.

append list m to the end of list n for all inputs.

B.

either cause a null pointer dereference or append list m to the end of list n.

C.

cause a null pointer dereference for all inputs.

D.

append list n to the end of list m for all inputs.
questions.correct_answer:
B.

either cause a null pointer dereference or append list m to the end of list n.
questions.explanation: Click here for detail solution by gateoverflow

questions.question:
Consider the following C code:

# include lt stdio.h >
int * assignval (int *x, int val) {
      *x = val;
      return x;
}
void main ( ) {
    int * x= malloc (sizeof (int));
    if (NULL = = x) return;
    x = assignval (x,0);
    if(x) { 
    x=(int *) malloc (sizeof (int));
    if (NULL = = x) return;
    x = assignval (x, 10);
}
printf("%d\n", *x);
free (x);
}
The code suffers from which one of the following problems:
options:
A.

compiler error as the return of malloc is not typecast appropriately.

B.

compiler error because the comparison should be made as x==NULL and not as shown.

C.

compiles successfully but execution may result in dangling pointer.

D.

compiles successfully but execution may result in memory leak.
questions.correct_answer:
D.

compiles successfully but execution may result in memory leak.

questions.question: Let A and B be infinite alphabets and let # be a symbol outside both A and B. Let f be a total functional from A* to B*. We say f is computable if there exists a Turning machine M which given an input x in A*, always halts with f(x) on its tape. Let \(L_{f}\) denote the language {x#f(x)|x\(\in\)A*}. Which of the following statements is true:
options:
A.

f is computable if and only if \(L_{f}\) is recursive.

B.

f is computable if and only \(L_{f}\) recursively enumerable.

C.

If f is computable then \(L_{f}\) is recursive, but not conversely.

D.

If f is computable then \(L_{f}\) is recursively enumerable, but not conversely.
questions.correct_answer:
A.

f is computable if and only if \(L_{f}\) is recursive.
questions.explanation: Click here for detail solution by gateoverflow

questions.question:
In a database system, unique time stamps are assigned to each transaction using Lamport's logical clock . Let TS(\(T_{1}\)) and TS(\(T_{2}\)) be the timestamps of transactions \(T_{1}\) and \(T_{2}\) respectively. Besides, \(T_{1}\) holds a lock on the resource R, and \(T_{2}\) has requested a conflicting lock on the same resource R. The following algorithm is used to prevent deadlocks in the database system assuming that a killed transaction is restarted with the same timestamp.

Assume any transactions that is not killed terminates eventually. Which of the following is TRUE about the database system that uses the above algorithm to prevent deadlocks?
options:
A.

The database system is both deadlock-free and starvation- free.

B.

The database system is deadlock- free, but not starvation-free.

C.

The database system is starvation-free but not deadlock- free.

D.

The database system is neither deadlock- free nor starvation-free.
questions.correct_answer:
A.

The database system is both deadlock-free and starvation- free.
questions.explanation: Click here for detail solution by gateoverflow
